mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which compounds corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher equity valuations over 20 trading days exclusively during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during recovery phases or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 100 basis points over 20 days.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate robust employment growth that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue forecasts, yielding positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently fails to predict short-term market direction with a Rank IC above 0.01 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation surpasses a 2.5 percent annual threshold, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in over 55% of periods where inflation breaches the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces GDP growth by more than 0.5 percentage points, it signals pent-up supply capacity that resolves into margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect yields negative returns in more than half of observed cases or loses predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales figures signal weakening consumer demand that immediately erodes corporate profit expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days specifically during high-interest-rate environments.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines fail to predict negative short-term returns during tightening cycles or produce a positive Rank IC greater than 0.04.
